name: Living-off-the-Land Binary Detection
description: Detects and investigates the abuse of legitimate Windows binaries (LOLBins) such as certutil, mshta, regsvr32, rundll32, and bitsadmin for malicious purposes. When a LOLBin execution with suspicious parameters is detected, Riggs AI analyzes the command line and process context to distinguish attacker abuse from legitimate usage. Confirmed abuse triggers process termination, host isolation, and an executive summary for incident tracking.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
required_integrations.reason: Kill malicious processes, retrieve process tree, and isolate endpoint

config.description: Triggers when a living-off-the-land binary is executed with suspicious parameters
Classify the LOLBin technique based on the binary and command line parameters

config.additional_instructions: Analyze this living-off-the-land binary execution for malicious intent. Consider the parent process chain, command line arguments, and whether the binary is being abused for download, execution, or defense evasion. Map to MITRE ATT&CK techniques.
config.description: AI-powered analysis of the LOLBin execution to determine if it represents attacker abuse or legitimate usage
Routes based on whether the AI determined the LOLBin execution is malicious
Terminate the malicious LOLBin process and isolate the endpoint

config.additional_instructions: Summarize the LOLBin abuse incident including the technique used, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, indicators of compromise, and containment actions taken.
config.description: Generate an executive summary of the LOLBin abuse incident for leadership reporting
Alert the SOC with LOLBin abuse details and executive summary

parameters.note: LOLBin execution of {{code_1.binary}} on {{trigger_1.alert.hostname}} reviewed. Technique: {{code_1.technique}}. Determined to be legitimate administrative usage.

config.description: Document that the LOLBin execution was legitimate and close the alert
